cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure out this question. The question is asking why weekend afternoons would become dead time without sports programs. The options are A to D. First, I need to remember the information given in the passage. The passage says that sports are used by TV companies to attract viewers during Saturday and Sunday afternoons, which are described as the slowest time periods for general TV viewing. So, without sports, those times would be considered "dead time". The passage mentions that sports are especially popular among male viewers who don't watch TV much at other times. So the key point here is that during weekends (Saturday and Sunday afternoons), TV viewing is normally low. Sports fill that gap by attracting viewers who otherwise wouldn't be watching. If there were no sports, those times would remain low in viewership, meaning there's not much audience. That would make it "dead time" because the TV networks can't sell advertising time during periods with low viewership. Looking at the options: A) Because there would be few viewers. That aligns with the idea that weekend afternoons are slow TV times. Without sports to attract viewers, fewer people would be watching, making it dead time. B) Advertisers off work? The passage doesn't mention advertisers being off work on weekends. It's more about viewership. C) TV programs would go slowly. I think this is a trick option. The passage says "slowest time periods for general television viewing", meaning fewer people watch. "Slowly" here might be confusing. But "dead time" refers to low ratings, not the pace of the programs themselves. D) Viewers would pay less. That's not mentioned.
